TextENGNew: A piece that drives home the lesson that whatever happens on this game board, it will all be attributed to the curse or to the Sonozaki family's machinations.
Or perhaps, to put it in my own way — this is the piece that opens everyone's eyes to the existence of Rule Z.
Though it's difficult to notice when looking at just this one piece.
...A tangled piece in which Shion Sonozaki, ensnared by Rule X and tossed about while mistaking it for Rule Y, gradually comes to recognize the existence of Rule Z.
And yet it is also an important piece that teaches us all the laws governing this game board.
changeReason: The new translation restores 'important' (重要な) and 'all' (全て) from the Japanese, which were omitted in the original, clarifying that this piece teaches ALL the laws of the game board and emphasizing its importance.

TextENGNew: ...Come to think of it, the piece called Shion Sonozaki may be counted, from the game board's perspective, as a piece that came from outside.
changeReason: The original states as fact that Shion 'is a piece coming from the outside,' but the Japanese (かもしれない) expresses uncertainty; the new translation correctly renders this as 'may be counted' as an outside piece.

TextENGNew: She initially despises Satoko Houjou — but through this piece and one other, she learns something precious, comes to resist Rule X, and grows into a formidable piece for the battle against Rule Z.
changeReason: The new translation more accurately renders 毛嫌い as 'despises' rather than 'dislikes,' significantly strengthening the depicted animosity, and corrects 'challenges' to 'resist' (抗え), better reflecting the defensive nature of Shion's stance against Rule X.

TextENGNew: What makes this piece so remarkable is precisely how cunningly that lesson is hidden — at a glance it looks nothing like what it truly is.
changeReason: The original's 'ridiculous' more closely matches the Japanese 滑稽 (comical/absurd), while the new translation's 'remarkable' shifts the tone from amusement at absurdity to admiration, potentially altering the reader's understanding of the speaker's attitude.

TextENGNew: Learn from your mistakes. Learn well.
And please — become a powerful piece and lend me the strength to vanquish my enemies...
